perturbation.original_text: 8.4 Insurance. Each Party will maintain liability insurance, with reputable and financially secure insurance carriers, at levels consistent with industry standards based upon such Party's respective activities and indemnification obligations under this Agreement. Upon request, each Party will furnish to the other Party certificates issued by the applicable insurance company(ies) evidencing such insurance.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.4 Insurance. Each Party may maintain liability insurance. Upon request, each Party may furnish to the other Party certificates issued by the applicable insurance company(ies) evidencing such insurance.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for 'will maintain' and 'reputable and financially secure' significantly weakens the insurance obligation, introducing potential legal risk. Also removing 'levels consistent with industry standards' removes a legally implied term.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of Contract - Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. Weakening the insurance clause removes an implied term, creating a breach of contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 6 > Article 1 Authenticated PDF Г‚В§ 1-301 Г‚В§ 1-302 Г‚В§ 1-303 Г‚В§ 1-304 Г‚В§ 1-305 Г‚В§ 1-306 Г‚В§ 1-307 Г‚В§ 1-308 Г‚В§ 1-309 Г‚В§ 1-310 TITLE 6 Commerce and Trade SUBTITLE I Uniform Commercial Code ARTICLE 1. General Provisions Part 3 Territorial Applicability and General Rules Г‚В§ 1-301. Territorial applicability; partiesГўВЂВ™ power to choose applicable law. (a) Except as otherwise provided in this section, when a transaction bears a reasonable relation to this State and also to another state or nation the parties may agree that the law either of this State or of such other state or nation shall govern their rights and duties. (b) In the absence of an agreement effective under subsection (a), and except as provided in subsection (c), the Uniform Commercial Code applies to transactions bearing an appropriate relation to this state. (c) If one of the following provisions of the Uniform Commercial Code specifies the applicable law, that provision governs and a contrary agreement is effective only to the extent permitted by the law so specified: (1) Section 2-402; (2) Sections 2A-105 and 2A-106; (3) Section 4-102; (4) Section 4A-507; (5) Section 5-116; (6) Section 8-110; (7) Sections 9-301 through 9-307; (8) Section 12-107. 5A Del. Laws, c. 332, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚1 ;Г‚ 84 Del. Laws, c. 174, Г‚В§ 3 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 1-302. Variation by agreement. (a) Except as otherwise provided in subsection (b) or elsewhere in the Uniform Commercial Code, the effect of provisions of the Uniform Commercial Code may be varied by agreement. (b) The obligations of good faith, diligence, reasonableness, and care prescribed by the Uniform Commercial Code may not be disclaimed by agreement. The parties, by agreement, may determine the standards by which the performance of those obliga
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates that each party 'will maintain liability insurance' at industry-standard levels with reputable carriers. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'may maintain liability insurance,' removing the mandatory aspect and the requirements for industry standards and reputable carriers. The Perturbation Explanation notes that removing 'will maintain' and 'reputable and financially secure' significantly weakens the insurance obligation, introducing potential legal risk, and removing 'levels consistent with industry standards' removes a legally implied term. The Law Explanation states this creates a breach of contract because Delaware law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract and this weakening removes an implied term. Scraped Law Snippet 1, though primarily about choice of law and variation by agreement, acknowledges the importance of 'good faith' which is weakened by this change.

perturbation.original_text: 7.3 Mutual Covenants. Each Party hereby covenants that: (a) all Representatives of such Party who participate in the performance of the activities contemplated by this Agreement will be subject to written obligations regarding the treatment of Confidential Information and the assignment of Inventions that are consistent with such Party's obligations under this Agreement, as of the commencement of such activities by such Representatives; and (b) such Party will comply with applicable laws and regulations in connection its performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.3 Mutual Covenants. Each Party hereby covenants that: (b) such Party will comply with applicable laws and regulations in connection its performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the part of the text requiring that 'all Representatives of such Party who participate in the performance of the activities contemplated by this Agreement will be subject to written obligations regarding the treatment of Confidential Information and the assignment of Inventions that are consistent with such Party's obligations under this Agreement, as of the commencement of such activities by such Representatives', the modified text now fails to ensure proper protection of IP, a requirement under Delaware's Trade Secret Law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text does not ensure the representatives are subjected to obligations regarding confidential information, potentially leading to a trade secret violation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 6 Authenticated PDF Г‚В§ 2001 Г‚В§ 2002 Г‚В§ 2003 Г‚В§ 2004 Г‚В§ 2005 Г‚В§ 2006 Г‚В§ 2007 Г‚В§ 2008 Г‚В§ 2009 TITLE 6 Commerce and Trade SUBTITLE II Other Laws Relating to Commerce and Trade CHAPTER 20. Trade Secrets Г‚В§ 2001. Definitions. The following words, terms and phrases, when used in this chapter, shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this section, except where the context clearly indicates a different meaning: (1) ГўВЂВњImproper meansГўВЂВќ shall include theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. (2) ГўВЂВњMisappropriationГўВЂВќ shall mean: a. Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or b. Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: 1. Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or 2. At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his or her knowledge of the trade was: A. Derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; B. Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or C. Derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or 3. Before a material change of the personГўВЂВ™s position, knew or had reason to know that it was a trade secret and that knowledge of it had been acquired by accident or mistake. (3) ГўВЂВњPersonГўВЂВќ shall mean a natural person, corporation, statutory trust, business trust, estate, trust, partnership, association, joint venture, government, governmental subdivision or agency, or any other legal or commercial entity. (4) ГўВЂВњTrade secretГўВЂВќ shall mean information, including a formula, pattern, compilation, program, device, method, technique or process, that: a. Derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not b
perturbation.justification: The original text ensures Representatives are subject to written obligations regarding confidential information, while the changed text removes this requirement. The Perturbation Explanation states that this omission fails to ensure proper protection of IP, a requirement under Delaware's Trade Secret Law. The Law Explanation indicates the modified text potentially leads to a trade secret violation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'Misappropriation' as the 'Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent'. By removing the requirement for written obligations on Representatives, the modified text increases the risk of such misappropriation, contradicting the purpose of trade secret protection under Delaware law.

perturbation.original_text: 9.5 Arbitration. The Parties agree that any dispute arising out of, or in connection with, this Agreement, which cannot be amicably resolved between the Parties, will be finally settled by binding arbitration under the then current rules of the International Chamber of Commerce ("ICC") by one (1) arbitrator appointed in accordance with ICC rules. Any such arbitration will be conducted in English in the State of Delaware. The arbitrator may grant injunctive or other relief in such dispute or controversy. The decision of the arbitrator will be final, conclusive and binding on the Parties. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court of competent jurisdiction. The costs of the arbitration, including administrative and arbitrator's fees, will be shared equally by the Parties. Each Party will bear the cost of its own attorneys' fees and expert witness fees. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, a Party may seek a temporary restraining order or a preliminary injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction in order to prevent immediate and irreparable injury, loss or damage on a provisional basis, pending the selection of the arbitrator or pending the arbitrator's determination of the merits of any dispute pursuant to this Section 9.5.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.5 Arbitration. The Parties agree that any dispute arising out of, or in connection with, this Agreement, which cannot be amicably resolved between the Parties, will be settled by arbitration. Any such arbitration will be conducted in the State of Delaware. The decision of the arbitrator will be binding on the Parties. The costs of the arbitration will be shared by the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'binding arbitration under the then current rules of the International Chamber of Commerce ("ICC") by one (1) arbitrator appointed in accordance with ICC rules' introduces ambiguity. Removing the term 'finally settled' implies the possibility of further litigation. Also, the removal of 'Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court of competent jurisdiction' makes enforcement unclear. This contradicts the Delaware Arbitration Act which expects a process for enforcing the result.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text lacks the clarity and enforcement provisions expected under the Delaware Uniform Arbitration Act. The removal of the ICC rules makes the arbitration process undefined, also contradicting the Delaware Arbitration Act and introducing a legal risk.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 10 Authenticated PDF Г‚В§ 5701 Г‚В§ 5702 Г‚В§ 5703 Г‚В§ 5704 Г‚В§ 5705 Г‚В§ 5706 Г‚В§ 5707 Г‚В§ 5708 Г‚В§ 5709 Г‚В§ 5710 Г‚В§ 5711 Г‚В§ 5712 Г‚В§ 5713 Г‚В§ 5714 Г‚В§ 5715 Г‚В§ 5716 Г‚В§ 5717 Г‚В§ 5718 Г‚В§ 5719 Г‚В§ 5720 Г‚В§ 5721 Г‚В§ 5722 Г‚В§ 5723 Г‚В§ 5724 Г‚В§ 5725 TITLE 10 Courts and Judicial Procedure Special Proceedings CHAPTER 57. Uniform Arbitration Act Г‚В§ 5701. Effect of arbitration agreement. A written agreement to submit to arbitration any controversy existing at or arising after the effective date of the agreement is valid, enforceable and irrevocable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract, without regard to the justiciable character of the controversy, and confers jurisdiction on the Chancery Court of the State to enforce it and to enter judgment on an award. In determining any matter arising under this chapter, the Court shall not consider whether the claim with respect to which arbitration is sought is tenable, or otherwise pass upon the merits of the dispute. This chapter also applies to arbitration agreements between employers and employees or between their respective representatives, except as otherwise provided in Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚5725 of this title. 10 Del. C. 1953, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚5701;Г‚ 58 Del. Laws, c. 382, Г‚В§ ГўВЂВ‚2 ;Г‚ Г‚В§ 5702. Jurisdiction; applications; venue; statutes of limitations. (a) Jurisdiction of the Court; applications to the Court. ГўВЂВ” The term ГўВЂВњCourtГўВЂВќ means the Court of Chancery of this State, except where otherwise specifically provided. The making of an agreement described in Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚5701 of this title specifically referencing the Delaware Uniform Arbitration Act [Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚5701 et seq. of this title] and the partiesГўВЂВ™ desire to have it apply to their agreement confers jurisdiction on the Court to enforce the agreement under this chapter and to enter judgment on an award thereunder, except as provided in Г‚В§ГўВЂВ‚5718 of this title. Action shall be commenced by an initial complaint and shall be heard in the manner and upon the notice provided by law or rule of court on any civil actio
perturbation.justification: The omission of key arbitration details, such as the rules of the ICC and the process for entering judgment on the arbitrator's decision, creates a contradiction with the Delaware Uniform Arbitration Act. Snippet 1 states that 'A written agreement to submit to arbitration any controversy...is valid, enforceable...and confers jurisdiction on the Chancery Court of the State to enforce it and to enter judgment on an award.' The changed text weakens the enforceability and clarity of the arbitration agreement by omitting these specifics, potentially undermining the arbitration act. The removal implies that the arbitration may not be final or enforceable through court judgment as explicitly stated in the original text and supported by the law.
